ICLR 2026 · arXiv 2510.15148

XModBench — how it works

One question, asked across every modality. A four-choice template, five task families, 61,320 items — and what current omni-models actually do with them.

scroll ↓
The template

Same question, six configurations

Each item is a four-choice multiple-choice question — a <context> (the question stem) and four <candidates> (answer options). Swap which modality carries the context and which carries the candidates, and one item becomes six.

V → T  (vision context, text candidates)
Which sound matches what is shown?
Adog howling
Bchicken clucking
Ccrocodile hissing
Dcuckoo bird calling
context = image  ·  4 candidates = text
V → A  (same image, audio candidates)
Which sound matches what is shown?
Adog howling
Bchicken clucking
Ccrocodile hissing
Dcuckoo bird calling
context = image  ·  4 candidates = audio clips
T → A  (text context, audio candidates)
Which sound matches what is shown?
[ a dog, mid-shot, outdoors ]
Adog howling
Bchicken clucking
Ccrocodile hissing
Dcuckoo bird calling
context = text  ·  4 candidates = audio clips
V→T🖼 Visioncontext → candidates🔤 Text
V→A🖼 Visioncontext → candidates🔊 Audio
T→A🔤 Textcontext → candidates🔊 Audio
T→V🔤 Textcontext → candidates🖼 Vision
A→V🔊 Audiocontext → candidates🖼 Vision
A→T🔊 Audiocontext → candidates🔤 Text
A <context> + four <candidates>; the modality of each is varied to form V→T, V→A, T→A, T→V, A→V, A→T. Identical semantics, six modality routings — isolating cross-modal consistency.
The tasks

Five task families, 17 subtasks

Broad coverage of what a model must actually understand — perception, spatial, temporal, linguistic and external-knowledge reasoning.

PerceptionWhat is it? — finegrained recognition, instruments, scenes
SpatialWhere / which direction? — 3D motion, arrangement, panorama
TemporalWhen & how many? — order, counting, calculation over time
LinguisticLinguistic — speech recognition & translation
KnowledgeExternal knowledge — music genre, movie, singer, emotion
The scale

Detailed statistics

Perception24,000
finegrained6,000
general_activities6,000
instruments6,000
instruments_comp3,000
natures3,000
Spatial7,776
3D_movements2,646
arrangements2,790
panorama2,340
Temporal9,000
calculation3,000
count3,000
order3,000
Linguistic8,244
recognition4,032
translation4,212
Knowledge12,300
emotion_classification4,200
movie_matching1,200
music_genre_classification6,000
singer_identification900
Total: 61,320 items  =  5 families · 17 subtasks · 6 configurations
The results

What models actually do

Full benchmark, 12 models (paper numbers). Spatial & temporal collapse; every model is systematically weaker on audio; directions of one pair disagree.

Task Competence Gaps

Per task family — spatial & temporal stay pinched for every model.

Modality Disparity

Bars drop below zero: models are systematically worse on audio.

Directional Imbalance

The two directions of one modality pair disagree.
